Title: Qualified Names
Description: Virtuoso supports multiple namespaces for tables and procedures. A table or procedure is uniquely identified using a three-part name consisting of qualifier, owner, and object name, separated by dots.
Each connection has a current qualifier. If an object reference does not specify a qualifier, the current qualifier is applied automatically.
The owner portion of the qualified name may be omitted if the resulting name remains unique (e.g., DB..SYS_KEYS).
Initially, all system objects use qualifier DB and owner DBA.
The default qualifier for all connections is DB unless changed using db.dba.user_set_qualifier.
Users may have a default qualifier assigned at login time. This is controlled through the stored procedure db.dba.user_set_qualifier.
